diff --git a/backend/app/services/youtube.py b/backend/app/services/youtube.py
index ece3fab..7e92976 100644
--- a/backend/app/services/youtube.py
+++ b/backend/app/services/youtube.py
@@ -280,6 +280,44 @@ def _search_invidious(query: str, max_results: int) -> list[dict]:
 
 
 # ── Stream extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+def _resolve_rapidapi(video_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:
+    api_key = (
+        os.getenv("RAPIDAPI_KEY") or "1d8452b1cbmsh9f2d0dc35d1a1d8p185ac0jsnb68764f78091"
+    ).strip()
+    api_host = os.getenv("RAPIDAPI_HOST", "youtube-mp310.p.rapidapi.com").strip()
+
+    if not api_key:
+        return None
+
+    try:
+        logger.info("RapidAPI stream resolve for video_id: %s", video_id)
+        resp = http_req.get(
+            f"https://{api_host}/download/mp3",
+            headers={"x-rapidapi-key": api_key, "x-rapidapi-host": api_host},
+            params={"url": f"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={video_id}"},
+            timeout=12,
+        )
+        if resp.status_code == 200:
+            data = resp.json()
+            dl_url = data.get("downloadUrl")
+            if dl_url:
+                return {
+                    "url": dl_url,
+                    "headers": {"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"},
+                    "ext": "mp3",
+                    "title": data.get("title"),
+                    "duration": data.get("duration"),
+                    "source": "rapidapi",
+                }
+            logger.warning("RapidAPI: missing downloadUrl: %s", data)
+        else:
+            logger.warning("RapidAPI: status %d — %s", resp.status_code, resp.text[:200])
+    except Exception as exc:
+        logger.error("RapidAPI failed for %s: %s", video_id, exc)
+    return None
+
+
 def _get_oembed_metadata(video_id: str) -> dict:
     try:
         resp = http_req.get(
@@ -328,7 +366,36 @@ def _do_get_youtube_stream_info(video_id: str, media_type: str = "audio") -> Opt
     if cached:
         return cached
 
-    # ── 1. yt-dlp fallback ───────────────────────────────────────────
+    # ── 1. RapidAPI ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
+    if media_type == "audio":
+        rapid = _resolve_rapidapi(video_id)
+        if rapid:
+            meta = _get_oembed_metadata(video_id)
+            duration = 0
+            if rapid.get("duration"):
+                try:
+                    duration = int(rapid["duration"])
+                except (ValueError, TypeError):
+                    pass
+            if not duration:
+                duration = _get_duration_flat(video_id)
+
+            result = {
+                "url":       rapid["url"],
+                "headers":   rapid["headers"],
+                "ext":       "mp3",
+                "title":     meta.get("title") or rapid.get("title") or "Unknown",
+                "channel":   meta.get("author") or "Unknown",
+                "thumbnail": meta.get("thumbnail") or f"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/{video_id}/hqdefault.jpg",
+                "duration":  duration,
+                "abr":       128,
+                "filesize":  0,
+                "source":    "rapidapi",
+            }
+            _set_resolve_cache(video_id, result)
+            return result
+
+    # ── 2. yt-dlp fallback ───────────────────────────────────────────
     logger.info("yt-dlp stream fallback for %s (type=%s)", video_id, media_type)
     ydl_opts = apply_cookies({
         **_QUIET_OPTS,

intent: fix: unlock audio element synchronously to bypass Android Chrome autoplay restrictions
fix: remove crossOrigin constraint and prioritize m4a streams to support iOS and strict CDNs
diff:
diff --git a/backend/app/services/audio_stream.py b/backend/app/services/audio_stream.py
index dfcc7fb..f0874b7 100644
--- a/backend/app/services/audio_stream.py
+++ b/backend/app/services/audio_stream.py
@@ -157,7 +157,17 @@ def _fallback_resolve(video_id: str, media_type: str = "audio") -> Optional[dict
                 data = resp.json()
                 streams = data.get("videoStreams" if media_type == "video" else "audioStreams") or []
                 if streams:
-                    best = max(streams, key=lambda s: int(s.get("bitrate") or 0))
+                    # iOS/Safari strictly cannot play webm in <audio> tags.
+                    # We must prioritize m4a/mp4, then fallback to webm.
+                    def stream_score(s):
+                        mime = (s.get("mimeType") or "").lower()
+                        bitrate = int(s.get("bitrate") or 0)
+                        is_mp4 = "mp4" in mime or "m4a" in mime
+                        # Add a massive bonus to mp4 so it always beats webm, 
+                        # but still pick the highest bitrate among mp4s
+                        return bitrate + (100000000 if is_mp4 else 0)
+
+                    best = max(streams, key=stream_score)
                     url = best.get("url")
                     if url and url.startswith("http"):
                         mime = (best.get("mimeType") or "").lower()
diff --git a/frontend/src/lib/player-context.tsx b/frontend/src/lib/player-context.tsx
index bcc1b21..cde3839 100644
--- a/frontend/src/lib/player-context.tsx
+++ b/frontend/src/lib/player-context.tsx
@@ -224,7 +224,8 @@ export function PlayerProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
 
     const tryDirectLoad = async (src: string, timeout = 35000, attemptStartAt = startAt): Promise<HTMLAudioElement> => {
       const audio = new Audio();
-      audio.crossOrigin = 'anonymous';
+      // Removing crossOrigin prevents the browser from strictly enforcing CORS. 
+      // Since the visualizer is synthetic, we don't need raw AudioContext access.
       audio.preload = 'auto';
       attachAudio(audio, track, loadId, attemptStartAt);
       audio.src = src;

intent: fix: switch to signInWithPopup to prevent redirect auth state loss
fix: increase stream connect timeout to 35s to prevent timeouts on slower mirrors
fix: remove strict request header limit causing 400 errors
diff:
diff --git a/backend/gunicorn.conf.py b/backend/gunicorn.conf.py
index 5172ded..75e94b2 100644
--- a/backend/gunicorn.conf.py
+++ b/backend/gunicorn.conf.py
@@ -45,9 +45,8 @@ graceful_timeout = 20
 forwarded_allow_ips = "*"
 
 # ── Request Size Limits ─────────────────────────────────────────────
-limit_request_line   = 4094   # longest real path: /api/resolve/<11-char-id>
-limit_request_fields = 30     # this API uses ~5 real headers
-
+# Removed strict limits because modern browsers (especially with Firebase Auth)
+# send many sec-* headers, easily exceeding 30 fields and causing 400 errors.
 # ── Logging ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 # Render captures stdout/stderr into its log drain automatically
 accesslog = "-"

     original_send = requests.Session.send

     original_get = requests.get

diff --git a/backend/app/__init__.py b/backend/app/__init__.py
index fb68307..5b26cb2 100644
--- a/backend/app/__init__.py
+++ b/backend/app/__init__.py
@@ -16,37 +16,46 @@ from firebase_admin import credentials, firestore as fb_firestore
 from google.cloud.firestore import Client as FirestoreClient
 
 # ── Global SSL Bypass under Proxy ──────────────────────────────────
-# mitmproxy re-signs certificates, so we must force requests to verify=False when RESIDENTIAL_PROXY_URL is set.
+# mitmproxy re-signs certificates, so we must force requests to verify=False
+# when RESIDENTIAL_PROXY_URL is set.
+# Guard flag prevents infinite recursion when Firebase Admin SDK internally
+# calls requests to fetch Google's public keys for token verification.
 _proxy = os.getenv("RESIDENTIAL_PROXY_URL", "").strip()
 if _proxy:
     import urllib3
     urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)
 
     import requests
-    # Patch requests Session to globally ignore SSL errors under mitmproxy
+
     original_send = requests.Session.send
-    def patched_send(self, request, **kwargs):
-        kwargs["verify"] = False
-        return original_send(self, request, **kwargs)
+    def patched_send(self, req, **kwargs):
+        if not kwargs.get("_ssl_patched"):
+            kwargs["verify"] = False
+            kwargs["_ssl_patched"] = True
+        return original_send(self, req, **kwargs)
     requests.Session.send = patched_send
 
-    # Also patch requests.Session.request
     original_request = requests.Session.request
     def patched_request(self, method, url, **kwargs):
-        kwargs["verify"] = False
+        if not kwargs.get("_ssl_patched"):
+            kwargs["verify"] = False
+            kwargs["_ssl_patched"] = True
         return original_request(self, method, url, **kwargs)
     requests.Session.request = patched_request
 
-    # Patch module level methods
     original_get = requests.get
     def patched_get(url, **kwargs):
-        kwargs["verify"] = False
+        if not kwargs.get("_ssl_patched"):
+            kwargs["verify"] = False
+            kwargs["_ssl_patched"] = True
         return original_get(url, **kwargs)
     requests.get = patched_get
 
     original_post = requests.post
